rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Loan disbursal is routed to wallets, cash, or third-party accounts instead of the borrower bank account
rules.plain_rule: Loan money must be sent directly to the borrower's own bank account, not to wallets or middlemen.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Repayments are routed through pass-through or wallet accounts rather than directly to the regulated entity
rules.plain_rule: Your repayments must go directly to the regulated lender, not through intermediate accounts.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Borrower is charged fees payable to the lending service provider or platform
rules.plain_rule: You should not be charged fees that go to the loan platform or app, not the actual lender.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022 (Annex I - KFS)
rules.description: Key Fact Statement is missing or explicitly disclaimed
rules.plain_rule: Every digital loan must include a Key Fact Statement summarising all key loan terms before you sign.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022 (Annex I - KFS)
rules.description: Annual Percentage Rate is not disclosed in an annualized form
rules.plain_rule: Your lender must tell you the full yearly cost of the loan, not just a daily or monthly rate.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2022, Para 6
rules.description: Cooling-off or look-up period is denied or restricted
rules.plain_rule: RBI gives you a window to cancel a digital loan after it is issued. Your lender cannot remove this right.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Lender reserves right to change rate unilaterally
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot change your interest rate whenever it wants without telling you first.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Auto-debit mandate is imposed without explicit, revocable borrower consent
rules.plain_rule: Automatic bank debits require your clear and separate consent, and you must be able to cancel them.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
pattern_keywords: credit limit may be increased automatically
rules.description: Credit limit is automatically increased without explicit borrower consent
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot increase your loan or credit limit automatically without asking you first.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2022, Para 12
rules.description: Loan app seeks access to prohibited phone resources such as contacts or call logs
rules.plain_rule: A loan app cannot demand access to your phone contacts, call logs, or photo gallery.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Borrower data can be shared with third parties without explicit consent
rules.plain_rule: Your personal and financial data cannot be shared with other companies without your clear permission.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Borrower consent is made irrevocable or non-withdrawable
rules.plain_rule: You must always be able to withdraw consent you have given, including data sharing consent.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Customer data is retained indefinitely without deletion or retention limits
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not keep your data forever without a valid reason and defined retention period.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Recovery practices allow harassment, threats, or public shaming
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot threaten, shame, or harass you or your family to collect repayments.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Collection practices allow contacting borrower contacts or references
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot contact your friends, family, or references to pressure you into repaying.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Grievance redressal contact or nodal officer details are missing
rules.plain_rule: Your lender must provide clear grievance redressal contact details in the loan agreement.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, 2022
rules.description: Regulated entity identity is unclear or can change without notice
rules.plain_rule: You must always know the name of the regulated lender giving you the loan, and it cannot change without notice.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2022 (Annex I - KFS)
rules.description: Loan agreement contains vague or undisclosed fees violating transparent disclosure norms
rules.plain_rule: All fees must be listed clearly upfront. Vague or open-ended charge clauses are not allowed.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
pattern_keywords: interest rate as per company policy
rules.description: Interest rate policy or methodology is not disclosed to the borrower
rules.plain_rule: Your NBFC lender must explain how your interest rate is set and when it can change.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Fees or charges are not transparently disclosed in the agreement
rules.plain_rule: All fees and charges must be listed clearly in your loan agreement before you sign.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Borrower is denied a copy of the loan agreement or sanction letter
rules.plain_rule: You must receive a copy of your signed loan agreement and sanction letter.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Repayment schedule is not fixed or disclosed upfront
rules.plain_rule: Your repayment schedule must be given to you before or at the time of loan disbursement.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Prepayment or foreclosure charges are discretionary or unclear
rules.plain_rule: Any prepayment charges must be clearly disclosed upfront and cannot be left to the lender's discretion.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Lender can recall or terminate the loan without notice or cause
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot demand immediate full repayment without a valid reason and proper notice.

rules.source: RBI/2023-24/53 (Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts)
rules.description: Penal charges are capitalized or compounded
rules.plain_rule: Penalty charges cannot be added to your principal balance and charged interest on top.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Collateral repossession is permitted without prior notice
rules.plain_rule: A lender must give proper notice before taking possession of any collateral or asset.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Recovery practices allow contact at unreasonable times or excessive calls
rules.plain_rule: Recovery agents must follow fair hours and respectful conduct when contacting borrowers.

rules.source: NBFC Master Direction 2016 (Fair Practices Code)
rules.description: Grievance redressal details are missing or disclaimed
rules.plain_rule: Your NBFC lender must provide a grievance redressal mechanism and officer contact details.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
pattern_keywords: co-lending arrangement need not be disclosed
rules.description: Co-lending arrangement or participating lenders are not disclosed to the borrower
rules.plain_rule: If multiple lenders are involved in your loan, all of them must be clearly disclosed to you.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
rules.description: Borrower is required to repay each co-lender separately
rules.plain_rule: Under a co-lending arrangement, you should have a single repayment process, not separate ones for each lender.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular 2020, Para 2.1
rules.description: Borrower must be quoted a single all-inclusive blended rate under co-lending arrangements
rules.plain_rule: In a co-lending loan, you must be given one single combined interest rate, not separate rates from each lender.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
rules.description: Co-lender can be changed without borrower notice or consent
rules.plain_rule: If the co-lender in your loan changes, you must be informed about it.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
rules.description: Borrower is charged a co-lending or partnership fee
rules.plain_rule: You should not be charged a separate fee just because multiple lenders are involved in your loan.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
pattern_keywords: not required to provide consolidated agreement
rules.description: Borrower is not provided a single consolidated loan agreement
rules.plain_rule: In a co-lending arrangement, you must receive one consolidated loan agreement covering all lenders.

rules.source: RBI Co-Lending Model Circular, 2020
rules.description: Co-lending disbursement or repayment is routed via a platform or service provider account
rules.plain_rule: Co-lending loan disbursements and repayments must not pass through a platform or service provider account.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: loan shall not be disbursed without collateral
tangible collateral to satisfaction of lender
rules.description: Detects clauses demanding collateral security from eligible micro or small enterprise borrowers for loan amounts up to the collateral-free threshold.
rules.plain_rule: Banks cannot force an eligible micro or small business to pledge property or other collateral for loans up to the RBI collateral-free limit.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: borrower shall not seek guarantee benefit
rules.description: Detects clauses attempting to contractually waive or suppress CGTMSE-backed collateral-free eligibility.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot make an eligible MSME give up collateral-free credit support available under approved guarantee schemes.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
rules.description: Detects coercive guarantee requirements imposed as substitutes for collateral on eligible collateral-free MSME borrowers.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not force an eligible MSME borrower to bring outside guarantors just to bypass collateral-free lending rules.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022
pattern_keywords: interest rate as determined by lender
rules.description: Detects undisclosed or unilateral pricing clauses without methodology, benchmark, or risk-based disclosure.
rules.plain_rule: Your loan agreement must clearly tell you how your interest rate is decided and when it can change.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022, Annex - Key Fact Statement; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
pattern_keywords: fees may be charged from time to time
rules.description: Detects hidden pricing or cost clauses not fully disclosed in the loan document or fact statement.
rules.plain_rule: All loan charges, not just EMI and interest, must be disclosed upfront before you accept the loan.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3; RBI circulars applicable to floating rate individual/MSME foreclosure practices
rules.description: Detects prepayment or foreclosure charges imposed contrary to disclosed borrower rights for eligible MSME products.
rules.plain_rule: If your MSME loan product is eligible for penalty-free closure, the lender cannot hide extra charges in the agreement.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022, Key Fact Statement requirements
pattern_keywords: fees not part of this agreement
closure cost determined at request date
rules.description: Detects undisclosed or externally referenced foreclosure conditions not disclosed in sanction terms.
rules.plain_rule: Prepayment rules must be shown in your loan papers before you sign, not added later.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), application acknowledgement and disposal provisions
rules.description: Detects contractual disclaimers that override prescribed acknowledgment or disposal timelines for MSME applications.
rules.plain_rule: Your MSME loan application must be acknowledged and processed within the lender's declared time limits.

rules.source: RBI Master Direction - Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - Targets and Classification, September 4, 2020; MSME Directions, Chapter III
pattern_keywords: lender may classify at its discretion
rules.description: Detects clauses obscuring or disclaiming regulatory classification of eligible MSME loans under priority sector norms.
rules.plain_rule: If your business qualifies as an MSME, the lender should classify the loan correctly under RBI priority sector rules.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment and monitoring provisions
rules.description: Detects one-sided diversion or misuse clauses that deny due process before recall or enforcement.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot accuse your business of fund misuse without a fair review process.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework and COVID-19 MSME restructuring circulars, 2020-2021
rules.description: Detects contractual waiver of statutory or regulatory restructuring access during stress events.
rules.plain_rule: Eligible MSME borrowers cannot be forced to give up restructuring options created under RBI relief frameworks.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
pattern_keywords: penalty shall form part of outstanding
rules.description: Detects capitalization or compounding of penal charges contrary to RBI penal charges framework.
rules.plain_rule: Penalty charges cannot be turned into new principal and charged interest again.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016; Credit Information Companies Act compliance norms
rules.description: Detects clauses denying notice, correction, or dispute rights before adverse credit reporting.
rules.plain_rule: You should be informed about credit reporting and allowed to dispute wrong reporting.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, application disposal and communication provisions
rules.description: Detects clauses disclaiming obligation to communicate rejection reasons to MSME applicants.
rules.plain_rule: If your MSME loan is rejected, the lender should tell you why.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital review and renewal provisions
rules.description: Detects clauses bypassing periodic review or renewal discipline for MSME working capital facilities.
rules.plain_rule: Working capital limits should be reviewed and renewed fairly and on time.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment norms
rules.description: Detects unilateral drawing power or margin recalculation clauses without transparent methodology.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should explain how your working capital limit and stock-based drawing power are calculated.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter II
rules.description: Detects clauses allowing lender-side override of statutory MSME classification or Udyam-based categorization.
rules.plain_rule: Your MSME category must follow government and RBI definitions, not the lender's private classification.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects tied-selling or bundled product clauses imposed without separate borrower consent.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot force your business to buy insurance, memberships, or other products just to get a loan.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: borrower shall not encumber any assets
negative lien over all present assets
borrower shall not create charge elsewhere
all movable assets shall remain unencumbered
borrower shall preserve assets for lender
asset disposal prohibited without lender approval
rules.description: Detects negative lien clauses used as substitute collateral in otherwise collateral-free MSME lending.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not use a disguised asset lock-up to defeat your collateral-free loan rights.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: fixed deposit shall be marked lien
rules.description: Detects cash or fixed deposit lien requirements imposed on eligible collateral-free MSME borrowers.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot replace property collateral with forced cash or fixed deposit security for an eligible collateral-free MSME loan.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024); CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: all existing securities shall secure this facility
rules.description: Detects cross-collateral clauses extending security to unrelated facilities in MSME lending.
rules.plain_rule: Your MSME loan should not automatically put other loans or assets into a common security pool without clear consent.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
pattern_keywords: interest may be revised from time to time
pricing may change without prior notice
rate revision shall be effective immediately
rules.description: Detects unilateral interest reset rights without borrower notice or disclosed methodology.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender must tell you how and when your interest rate can change.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022, Key Fact Statement
rules.description: Detects hidden finance costs excluded from APR or key fact disclosure.
rules.plain_rule: All loan costs must be disclosed upfront, not hidden under technology or platform fees.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects clauses reducing disbursement by collecting interest in advance without transparent disclosure.
rules.plain_rule: The lender should not quietly deduct interest from your loan before you receive the money.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Para 6
pattern_keywords: funds may be routed through platform account
rules.description: Detects disbursement or repayment through unauthorized pass-through accounts.
rules.plain_rule: Loan money should move directly between you and the regulated lender, not through middleman accounts.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex
rules.description: Detects invasive data collection clauses beyond permissible digital lending data access.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot force your business to share your contacts, messages, or private device data just to get a loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022; Key Fact Statement requirements
rules.description: Detects automatic debit mandates imposed without separate informed consent.
rules.plain_rule: Automatic debit from your bank account requires clear and separate consent.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
rules.description: Detects unreasonable lock-in periods preventing early repayment.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should clearly disclose if there is any lock-in period before loan closure.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
rules.description: Detects penal charges renamed as service fees to evade RBI restrictions.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot disguise penalties as service or monitoring fees when you miss a payment.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
rules.description: Detects multiple overlapping default charges for the same event.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not pile multiple penalties on the same missed payment.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, application acknowledgement provisions
pattern_keywords: application may be processed without acknowledgment
rules.description: Detects denial of acknowledgment for MSME loan applications.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should give you proof that your MSME loan application was received.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital norms
pattern_keywords: statement may be treated as invalid
rules.description: Detects clauses allowing arbitrary rejection of stock or inventory statements.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should use transparent rules when checking your stock statements for working capital limits.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment norms
pattern_keywords: margin may be increased at any time
rules.description: Detects unilateral margin hikes without transparent methodology.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should explain why your working capital margin is being increased.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework and COVID-19 MSME restructuring circulars, 2020-2021
rules.description: Detects automatic acceleration clauses that may defeat restructuring rights.
rules.plain_rule: Temporary business stress should not automatically cancel your right to seek restructuring.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects adverse credit reporting despite unresolved borrower disputes.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not report disputed amounts as final defaults without proper review.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
pattern_keywords: settled status may not be reported
rules.description: Detects clauses denying obligation to update credit bureau after repayment or settlement.
rules.plain_rule: When your loan is closed, your lender should update your credit record correctly.

rules.source: RBI Master Direction - Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - Targets and Classification, September 4, 2020
rules.description: Detects clauses reclassifying eligible MSME exposures outside priority sector treatment.
rules.plain_rule: If your business qualifies as MSME, your lender should not hide or remove its priority sector status.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects tied-selling of insurance or affiliate products without independent consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot force your business to buy insurance from its own group company to get a loan.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects coercive account migration or tied banking arrangements.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not force your business to shift all banking relationships just to get a loan.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, recovery provisions
pattern_keywords: lender may seize assets without notice
rules.description: Detects recovery or repossession clauses eliminating notice and fair recovery process.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot seize business assets without following a fair and notified recovery process.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Recovery Policy; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022
rules.description: Detects public naming, reputational pressure, or shaming tactics used for recovery.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot shame your business publicly to force repayment.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Recovery Policy
rules.description: Detects clauses permitting lender recovery contact with unrelated third parties.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not contact your customers, suppliers, or family to pressure repayment.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
rules.description: Detects punitive recovery visit charges imposed on delinquent borrowers.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot turn collection visits into hidden penalties or default charges.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Recovery Policy
rules.description: Detects unrestricted recovery contact clauses violating fair collection norms.
rules.plain_rule: Recovery calls and visits must follow fair timing and respectful conduct.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital financing norms
rules.description: Detects excessive receivables control that functions as undisclosed security or operational restriction.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not quietly take over all your customer collections without clear disclosure and necessity.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment norms
rules.description: Detects arbitrary exclusion of receivables from drawing power calculations.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should use transparent rules when excluding receivables from your working capital limit.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital review provisions
rules.description: Detects facility freeze clauses without objective review criteria or borrower notice.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not freeze your working capital line without clear reasons and communication.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework for COVID-19-related Stress, 2020-2021; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects waiver of cure periods that may undermine restructuring or remediation rights.
rules.plain_rule: Your business should usually get a fair chance to fix a breach before harsh enforcement action.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework and MSME restructuring circulars, 2020-2021
pattern_keywords: restructuring shall not affect NPA classification
rules.description: Detects clauses preserving adverse classification despite approved restructuring.
rules.plain_rule: If your MSME loan is restructured under RBI rules, the lender should follow RBI classification rules, not override them by contract.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework; RBI due process principles
pattern_keywords: borrower may be marked wilful defaulter
credit file may reflect wilful default
rules.description: Detects wilful defaulter labeling without due process or hearing.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot label your business as a wilful defaulter without proper process and evidence.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, application disposal provisions
rules.description: Detects clauses preventing grievance or review after loan rejection.
rules.plain_rule: You should be able to ask questions or raise complaints if your MSME loan is rejected.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, application processing norms
rules.description: Detects silent expiry or abandonment of loan applications.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should tell you if your MSME loan application is being closed or expired.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: security top up at lender discretion
rules.description: Detects open-ended collateral enhancement triggers on eligible MSME borrowers.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not reserve unlimited rights to demand extra collateral from an eligible collateral-free MSME borrower.

rules.source: CGTMSE Operational Guidelines; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects guarantees that remain enforceable after full loan discharge.
rules.plain_rule: Once your MSME loan is fully repaid, related guarantees should not continue forever.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex
pattern_keywords: data may be shared with affiliates
third parties may use borrower data
rules.description: Detects default or bundled consent for sharing borrower data with affiliates or third parties.
rules.plain_rule: Your business data cannot be automatically shared with affiliates or partners without clear consent.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects outsourced recovery clauses lacking borrower disclosure and accountability.
rules.plain_rule: If recovery is outsourced, your lender should clearly tell you who is contacting you and why.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects tied-selling of payment acceptance or gateway products with MSME loans.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not force your business to buy payment products just to get a loan.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects software or SaaS subscriptions tied to credit sanction without separate consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot make your business buy software subscriptions just to receive a loan.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, Chapter II
rules.description: Detects contractual rejection of valid statutory MSME registration.
rules.plain_rule: A valid Udyam registration should be respected when determining your MSME eligibility.

rules.source: RBI Master Direction - Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - Targets and Classification, September 4, 2020
rules.description: Detects contractual suppression or disclaimer of priority sector treatment for eligible MSME loans.
rules.plain_rule: If your business qualifies, your lender should not hide or waive the regulatory benefits of MSME priority sector classification.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022, Key Fact Statement
pattern_keywords: interest computed as per internal formula
finance charges calculated by proprietary model
rules.description: Detects hidden or undisclosed interest calculation methodology.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender must clearly explain how interest is calculated on your loan.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
rules.description: Detects undisclosed day-count conventions that can increase effective borrowing cost.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should tell you if interest is calculated using special day-count rules that affect your cost.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
rules.description: Detects retrospective interest repricing clauses.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not increase your loan rate for past periods without clear and fair disclosure.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
pattern_keywords: sanction not required for fee retention
charges non refundable under any circumstances
rules.description: Detects unconditional retention of fees before sanction or service delivery.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should clearly justify any non-refundable fees charged before your loan is approved.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022, Key Fact Statement; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
pattern_keywords: borrower deemed to accept online policies
rules.description: Detects loan agreements that hide material terms in external links or future documents.
rules.plain_rule: All important loan terms should be in your signed loan documents, not hidden on a website.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code, Para 3; RBI MSME Lending Directions
pattern_keywords: pricing may increase upon revenue decline
rules.description: Detects default-linked or stress-linked repricing disguised as commercial adjustment.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not quietly increase your interest just because your business is temporarily under stress.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023; MSME Working Capital Norms
rules.description: Detects recurring inspection fees that may operate as hidden penalties or unfair charges.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not use inspections as a hidden way to increase your borrowing cost.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53; MSME Working Capital Assessment Norms
rules.description: Detects punitive fees for operational reporting defaults.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not convert routine reporting delays into hidden penalty income without fair disclosure.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital review provisions
rules.description: Detects arbitrary seasonal reduction of sanctioned working capital limits.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should explain and document any reduction in your working capital limit.

rules.source: RBI MSME Lending Directions; Fair Practices Code
pattern_keywords: lender may collect from debtors directly
rules.description: Detects automatic assignment of receivables without explicit borrower consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not take control of your customer invoices without clear and informed consent.

rules.source: RBI MSME Lending Directions; Trade Receivables Discounting System framework
rules.description: Detects contractual restrictions on legitimate receivables financing mechanisms.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not block your MSME from using approved receivables financing platforms.

rules.source: RBI COVID-19 Regulatory Package, 2020; Resolution Framework
rules.description: Detects contractual waiver of RBI-granted moratorium or payment relief rights.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot force your business to give up RBI-approved payment relief rights.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework and MSME restructuring circulars, 2020-2021
pattern_keywords: restructuring requests shall not be entertained
rules.description: Detects blanket denial of restructuring applications from eligible MSMEs.
rules.plain_rule: Eligible MSMEs should be allowed to apply for restructuring during financial stress.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects adverse bureau reporting without prior borrower communication.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should warn you before reporting a default to a credit bureau.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework
rules.description: Detects clauses waiving borrower rights to dispute or correct credit records.
rules.plain_rule: You should be able to challenge and correct wrong credit reporting.

rules.source: CGTMSE Operational Guidelines; Fair Practices Code
pattern_keywords: all promoter assets shall be disclosed
rules.description: Detects excessive personal asset disclosure requirements used to pressure MSME promoters.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should only ask for personal financial information that is genuinely needed for credit assessment.

rules.source: CGTMSE Operational Guidelines; RBI MSME Lending Directions
pattern_keywords: family member shall act as guarantor
rules.description: Detects coercive family guarantee requirements for eligible borrowers.
rules.plain_rule: An eligible MSME borrower should not be forced to bring family members as guarantors just to access credit.

rules.source: CGTMSE Operational Guidelines; Fair Practices Code
pattern_keywords: guarantee for all present and future obligations
rules.description: Detects unlimited personal guarantees extending beyond the sanctioned exposure.
rules.plain_rule: Personal guarantees should not expose promoters to unlimited and indefinite liability without clear limits.

rules.source: RBI Master Direction - Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - Targets and Classification, September 4, 2020
rules.description: Detects structuring designed to avoid correct PSL classification of MSME exposure.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not split your loan into pieces to avoid giving it proper MSME regulatory treatment.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects bundled affiliate sales without transparent separation of loan and commission-based products.
rules.plain_rule: You should know when your lender is selling you extra products for commission, not as part of the loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Data Collection and Consent
rules.description: Detects clauses authorizing collection or upload of personal contact lists from borrower devices.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot demand access to your phone contacts to approve or collect a business loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Data Collection and Consent
rules.description: Detects clauses allowing SMS scraping or analysis for underwriting or recovery.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot scan your phone messages to decide or recover your MSME loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Data Collection and Consent
rules.description: Detects invasive geolocation tracking clauses unrelated to core credit operations.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot continuously track your location just because you took a business loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Data Collection and Consent
rules.description: Detects clauses requiring unnecessary access to borrower images or device media.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot force access to your photos or camera for a standard MSME loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Data Collection and Consent
rules.description: Detects clauses requiring microphone access or audio capture unrelated to legitimate loan servicing.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot ask to listen through your phone to provide a business loan.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Recovery Policy
rules.description: Detects workplace pressure tactics targeting employees or business premises.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not pressure your staff or workplace to recover your loan.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Recovery Policy
rules.description: Detects clauses allowing lenders to contact customers to create repayment pressure.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not threaten your business reputation by contacting your customers about your loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects recovery through social media, messaging platforms, or digital public channels.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot chase your business on social media or messaging apps to shame you into paying.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
pattern_keywords: penalty at x percent of outstanding
rules.description: Detects penal charges structured as interest-like percentage charges on outstanding balances.
rules.plain_rule: Penalty charges should not behave like extra interest on your loan balance.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
pattern_keywords: penalty for each day of delay
rules.description: Detects daily accruing penal structures that resemble compounding interest.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not make penalties grow every day like hidden interest unless clearly lawful and disclosed.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital review provisions
pattern_keywords: working capital may be restructured automatically
CC facility may be frozen and converted
limit may be converted without consent
rules.description: Detects unilateral conversion of revolving working capital facilities into term loans.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not convert your working capital line into a term loan without clear notice and consent.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code, Para 3; MSME Working Capital Norms
rules.description: Detects charging of interest on unutilized working capital exposure without proper disclosure.
rules.plain_rule: You should not pay interest on money your business never actually used unless clearly disclosed and justified.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment norms
rules.description: Detects retroactive drawing power recalculation leading to surprise defaults or charges.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not recalculate old working capital limits later and suddenly call your business in default.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, Chapter II
rules.description: Detects structuring or classification language used to distort statutory MSME status.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should follow RBI and government rules for enterprise grouping, not split businesses to deny MSME benefits.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, Chapter II
rules.description: Detects refusal to recognize updated Udyam or statutory classification changes.
rules.plain_rule: If your business category changes under official MSME rules, your lender should update its records.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
pattern_keywords: premium shall form part of facility
rules.description: Detects automatic financing of bundled insurance without informed consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot quietly add insurance costs to your loan principal without your clear approval.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects tied-selling of merchant hardware or payment terminals.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not force your business to lease or buy payment devices to get credit.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects forced opening of accounts with group entities or affiliates as a condition for credit.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not force your business into extra banking relationships with group companies just to get a loan.

rules.source: RBI Master Direction - Priority Sector Lending (PSL) - Targets and Classification, September 4, 2020
pattern_keywords: borrower has no right to PSL reporting
rules.description: Detects contractual attempts to suppress or disclaim regulatory PSL reporting treatment.
rules.plain_rule: If your MSME loan qualifies for priority sector treatment, the lender should not hide that classification.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
rules.description: Detects composite loan structures bundling non-credit products without separate borrower consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot hide extra paid products inside a loan package without your clear and separate approval.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024), Chapter IV, Para 4.1; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
pattern_keywords: all receipts shall be routed through escrow
business collections shall be ring fenced
rules.description: Detects escrow or cash trap arrangements used as disguised collateral for eligible collateral-free MSME facilities.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not use a forced escrow arrangement to quietly replace your collateral-free loan rights.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector] Directions, 2017 (Updated June 11, 2024); CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
rules.description: Detects blanket floating charges over future assets that may defeat collateral-free protections.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not automatically claim every asset your business may acquire in the future just to secure an eligible MSME loan.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code, Para 3; CGTMSE Operational Guidelines
rules.description: Detects undisclosed fees for modifying, releasing, or substituting security arrangements.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should clearly disclose any genuine charges for changing security documents and not use them as hidden revenue.

rules.source: Master Direction - Non-Banking Financial Company â€“ Fair Practices Code (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, Para 3
rules.description: Detects asymmetric rounding conventions that can systematically increase borrower cost.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should clearly disclose how interest or charges are rounded so you are not overcharged silently.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code, Para 3; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, Key Fact Statement
pattern_keywords: EMI may be revised without notice
rules.description: Detects unilateral EMI or repayment schedule changes without borrower notice.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should tell you before changing your EMI or repayment schedule.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Annex - Key Fact Statement
rules.description: Detects waiver or exclusion of mandatory Key Fact Statement disclosures.
rules.plain_rule: You must receive a clear summary of your loan costs and terms before accepting a digital loan.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Para 6
pattern_keywords: disbursement may be made to vendor
loan amount may be routed externally
rules.description: Detects unauthorized third-party disbursement structures outside permitted exceptions.
rules.plain_rule: Your loan money should normally come directly to you or your approved end-use destination under RBI rules, not through hidden intermediaries.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects clauses preventing borrowers from revoking recurring payment mandates.
rules.plain_rule: You should be able to cancel automatic debit instructions according to lawful banking procedures.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code, Recovery Policy; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022
rules.description: Detects outsourced field recovery clauses lacking identity disclosure or accountability language.
rules.plain_rule: If someone visits your business for recovery, you should know exactly who they are and whom they represent.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
rules.description: Detects penal charges disguised as interest rate repricing after default.
rules.plain_rule: A lender cannot disguise penalties as a higher interest rate after you miss a payment.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
pattern_keywords: penalty in addition to penal charges
rules.description: Detects stacking of multiple charges for the same delinquency event.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not charge your business multiple penalties for the same missed payment.

rules.source: RBI MSME Working Capital Norms; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects forced use of lender-affiliated auditors or inspection firms.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not force your business to hire its own affiliate for audits unless clearly justified.

rules.source: Reserve Bank of India [Lending to MSME Sector] Directions, working capital assessment norms
rules.description: Detects black-box or undisclosed algorithmic determination of working capital availability.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should explain how your working capital limit is calculated, even if software is used.

rules.source: Fair Practices Code; RBI Resolution Framework
pattern_keywords: technical breach constitutes event of default
rules.description: Detects harsh acceleration triggered by minor technical covenant breaches.
rules.plain_rule: Small paperwork or ratio breaches should not automatically make your entire loan immediately payable.

rules.source: RBI Resolution Framework for COVID-19-related Stress, 2020-2021; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects potentially unfair fees for accessing restructuring or resolution mechanisms.
rules.plain_rule: A lender should not create unreasonable charges just for your business to request restructuring during hardship.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects adverse credit reporting before a reasonable cure or communication period.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not rush to damage your credit record before giving your business a fair chance to correct a missed payment.

rules.source: Credit Information Companies compliance framework
rules.description: Detects clauses disclaiming obligation to correctly report settled or closed accounts.
rules.plain_rule: When your MSME loan is settled or closed, your credit report should be updated accurately.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code, Para 3
pattern_keywords: coverage mandatory unless declined in writing
rules.description: Detects opt-out blocking or pre-selected insurance sales bundled with loans.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot automatically enroll your business in insurance and make opting out difficult.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Fair Practices Code
rules.description: Detects lock-in periods for non-credit merchant or software services bundled with loans.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not lock your business into unrelated subscriptions just because you took a loan.

rules.source: RBI Fair Practices Code; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; RBI MSME Lending Directions
rules.description: Detects broad waiver clauses attempting to contract out of RBI-mandated borrower protections.
rules.plain_rule: No loan agreement can make your business give up protections that RBI rules already guarantee.

rules.source: Master Direction - NBFC Fair Practices Code, Recovery and Repossession Policy
pattern_keywords: assets may be taken without intimation
rules.description: Detects repossession clauses bypassing notice, communication, or due process.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot seize business assets without proper notice and fair recovery procedures.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Data Storage and Retention
rules.description: Detects open-ended retention of borrower data without defined purpose or retention period.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not keep your business data forever without a valid reason and clear retention rules.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Cooling-off / Look-up Period
rules.description: Detects fees charged when borrowers exercise RBI-mandated cooling-off exit rights.
rules.plain_rule: If RBI gives you a cooling-off period, your lender cannot punish your business for using it.

rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending, September 2, 2022, Cooling-off / Look-up Period
pattern_keywords: loan once accepted cannot be rescinded
rules.description: Detects clauses eliminating borrower cancellation or cooling-off rights.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot make your business give up RBI-protected cancellation rights.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, Payment Mandates
rules.description: Detects cumulative charges for repeated debit retries on the same missed payment.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not charge your business again and again for retrying the same failed payment.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
rules.description: Detects stacking of bounce charges and penal charges for the same missed payment event.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender should not punish your business twice for the same failed payment.

rules.source: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines, 2022; Payment Mandate Controls
pattern_keywords: account changes shall not affect authorization
debit authority extends to future accounts
rules.description: Detects clauses extending payment mandates to future or replacement bank accounts without fresh consent.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot automatically extend debit rights to new bank accounts without your approval.

rules.source: RBI Circular RBI/2023-24/53 - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts, August 18, 2023
pattern_keywords: interest shall increase for delayed stock statements
rules.description: Detects penal charges disguised as interest rate increases for delayed operational reporting.
rules.plain_rule: Your lender cannot punish your business with higher interest just because paperwork was delayed.
